findings.summary: generate_session_key reseeds Python's non-cryptographic random module with int(time.time()) on every call, then derives the 32-byte session key from that deterministic PRNG. An attacker who can estimate the generation time to within a few seconds can brute-force the timestamp seed and recover the exact key; calls made in the same second also repeat the same session key.
findings.evidence: Running python3 artifacts/repro/predict_time_seeded_key.py recovered the exact observed 32-byte key after searching 11 candidate timestamp seeds and showed that a second same-second call produced the identical key.
